MSNeuroLab 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

stratified_distance_analysis

stratified_distance_analysis.Rd

stratified_distance_analysis This function performs stratified distance matching between patients and controls based on multiple variables. It identifies the closest control group for each patient group using distance-based metrics across defined strata.

Usage

stratified_distance_analysis(
  data = mtcars,
  brain_cols = c("mpg", "cyl", "hp"),
  group_var = "am",
  patient_value = 1,
  control_value = 0,
  strat_var = "gear",
  distance_method = "euclidean",
  profile_method = "mean",
  plot_title = NULL,
  include_counts = TRUE
)

Arguments

data

data.frame that contains numeric or categorical variables arranged in columns.

brain_cols

List of columns that are used in computing distance metrics.

group_var

Column containing categories for the two groups whose distance is being analysed, for example MS patient and healthy control.

patient_value

Value in group_var that identifies patients. Every patient group is matched to the closest control group.

control_value

Value in group_var that identifies controls.

strat_var

Stratification variable. Column indicating subgroups in between witch distance is assessed and matching is done. Factorize to determine plotting order.

distance_method

Metric used to compute difference values. Choose "euclidean", "cosine", or "manhattan".

profile_method

Method to calculate group profiles: Should difference be determined between mean or median of individuals in strata. Choose "mean" or "median".

plot_title

Plot title. Will be auto-generated if NULL.

include_counts

Whether to include subject counts in results table.

Value

warnings

Non-fatal warnings

results

Data frame with distance metrics for all patient-control strata combinations

top_matches

Filtered results showing only closest matches for each patient stratum

heatmap

Heatmap plot showing distance between all patient and control strata

gridplot

Grid plot showing only closest matches

Examples
